qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Sensitive encryption key disclosure in backup API response
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes encryption key disclosure in the backup API endpoint, which is unrelated to the advisory's root cause of disabled users retaining API access through unrevoked tokens. The vulnerable code locations (api/backup/backup.go) and missing status check in token validation (internal/user/user.go) are different components and root causes.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Site Health Check functionality
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding describes SSRF via the site health check functionality, which is unrelated to the advisory's root cause of disabled user token persistence. The vulnerable code locations (internal/sitecheck/checker.go, api/sites/sitecheck.go) and missing status check in token validation (internal/user/user.go) are different components and root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the two qualifying findings identify the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory (GHSA-x234-x5vq-cc2v) describes a specific authentication/authorization vulnerability where token-based authentication in internal/user/user.go (lines 44-139) fails to check the user.Status field, allowing disabled users to retain full API access through previously issued bearer tokens. The qualifying findings describe different vulnerabilities in different components: (1) encryption key disclosure in the backup API, and (2) SSRF via site health check functionality. None of these findings match the advisory's root cause of missing status validation during token-based authentication.
evidence: Advisory root cause: Token validation in internal/user/user.go lines 44-139 does not check user.Status field
Advisory impact: Disabled users retain full API access through previously issued tokens
Finding 1 (backup_token_disclosure): Vulnerable code at api/backup/backup.go:23,35 - unrelated to token validation
Finding 2 (ssrf_site_checker): Vulnerable code at internal/sitecheck/checker.go:356, api/sites/sitecheck.go:147 - unrelated to user status checks
No finding mentions user.Status, token validation bypass for disabled accounts, or internal/user/user.go authentication logic
